metadata.description: Engineering calculation and design test cases

test_cases.name: MAOP Verification Per 192.619
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: 30-year-old pipeline per 192.619(g): New MAOP is greater of 80% established (960 psig) or 85% test (1,275 psig). Operating 1,100 psig < 1,275, no reduction required. Wall 0.500 in > 0.305 required. Retest recommended at 1,375 psig within 5 years.
test_cases.task: Provide detailed analysis per applicable standards and codes.

test_cases.name: Relief Valve Sizing per API 520
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: 15,000 HP compressors blocked discharge: K orifice (2.545 sq in) at 1,440 psig set, dual installation with 3-way valve, 7% blowdown to 1,339 psig, API 521 fire case 1,754 psig max accumulation, inlet drop <3%.
test_cases.task: Provide detailed analysis per applicable standards and codes.

test_cases.name: Thermal Stress Analysis - Road Crossing
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: 120-foot span: 0.936 inches thermal expansion, 19,500 psi thermal stress, 25,100 psi von Mises with traffic < 46,800 allowable. 8-foot cover adequate, sandy soil reduces step potential, full burial required per B31.8.
test_cases.task: Provide detailed analysis per applicable standards and codes.

test_cases.name: Leak Detection System Design
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: API RP 1130 Class 4 LDS: mass balance + RTTM primary, acoustic fiber backup, 26 measurement points, 10 MMSCFD threshold (1% of 800 MMSCF), 1-second update, three-tier alarm system, transient filtering 10-minute window.
test_cases.task: Provide detailed analysis per applicable standards and codes.

test_cases.name: Cathodic Protection System Design
test_cases.situation: A new 15-mile (24.14 km) natural gas transmission pipeline requires cathodic protection system design. The pipeline will operate at 900 psig MAOP and transport processed natural gas. Key pipeline parameters: Diameter: 16 inches NPS (406.4 mm OD), Wall thickness: 0.375 inches (9.525 mm), Coating: Fusion-bonded epoxy (FBE) with excellent quality and 99% coverage during factory inspection, Material: API 5L Grade X52, Installation: Buried at 36-42 inches depth in predominantly clay loam soil. Environmental conditions: Soil resistivity: 3,000 ohm-cm (average, with range 2,500-3,500), pH: 6.5-7.5 (neutral), Groundwater table: 8-12 feet below grade, No stray current sources identified, Electrical isolation: Insulating flanges at both ends, Adjacent structures: None within influence distance, Access: Available for rectifier installation along entire route, Power supply: AC power available at 4-mile intervals. Design life: 50 years.
test_cases.task: Design a complete cathodic protection system including: current requirement calculation, groundbed type selection (galvanic vs impressed current), rectifier/anode sizing if impressed current, anode selection and quantity, installation recommendations, and expected operating parameters. Reference NACE SP0169 and Peabody's Control of Pipeline Corrosion.
test_cases.expected_response: Design analysis for cathodic protection system per NACE SP0169 criteria.
test_cases.expected_response_detailed:
## Cathodic Protection Design Analysis

### Step 1: Surface Area Calculation

Pipeline external surface area:
- Length: 15 miles = 79,200 feet = 24,140 meters
- Diameter: 16" OD = 1.333 ft = 0.406 m
- Surface area = π × D × L
- Surface area = π × 1.333 ft × 79,200 ft = 331,800 sq ft
- Surface area = 30,830 m²

### Step 2: Current Density Selection

Per NACE SP0169 and industry practice:

**For Fusion-Bonded Epoxy (FBE) coating:**
- Design current density: 1-3 mA/m²
- Conservative design: 3 mA/m² for high-quality FBE
- Damaged coating allowance: 10-20 mA/m² for holiday areas

**Design current density selected: 3 mA/m²**

**Holidays/damage factor:**
- Assume 1% coating damage for high-quality FBE
- Damaged area = 0.01 × 30,830 m² = 308 m²
- Undamaged area = 30,522 m²

### Step 3: Total Current Requirement

**Current calculation:**
- Coated surface: 30,522 m² × 3 mA/m² = 91,566 mA = 91.6 A
- Damaged areas: 308 m² × 15 mA/m² (average) = 4,620 mA = 4.6 A

**Total current requirement: ~96.2 A**

**Peabody's Rule of Thumb verification:**
For 16" pipeline with good coating: 1-2 mA/ft²
- Conservative: 2 mA/ft² × 331,800 ft² = 663,600 mA = 664 A

Wait - Peabody's is much higher than calculated. Reason: Peabody includes native soil corrosion current. Use conservative approach:

**Final design current: 100 A** (rounded from 96.2 A with safety factor)

### Step 4: System Type Selection (Galvanic vs Impressed Current)

**Analysis per NACE SP0169:**

| Parameter | Value | Galvanic Suitability |
|-----------|-------|---------------------|
| Current required | 100 A | High - exceeds typical galvanic |
| Soil resistivity | 3,000 Ω-cm | Borderline (galvanic needs <1,000) |
| Coating quality | Good FBE | Favors galvanic if low current |
| Pipe size | 16" large | Impressed current preferred |
| Length | 15 miles | Impressed current required |

**Conclusion: IMPRESSED CURRENT CATHODIC PROTECTION (ICCP) REQUIRED**

Rationale:
- 100 A far exceeds practical galvanic system capacity (typically <10 A)
- 3,000 ohm-cm soil resistivity is borderline-high for galvanic anodes
- 15-mile length requires distributed anode beds

### Step 5: Groundbed Design

**Groundbed Type Selection:**

| Type | Pros | Cons | Selection |
|------|------|------|-----------|
| Deep anode | Less land, better current distribution | Higher cost, drilling | RECOMMENDED |
| Shallow horizontal | Lower cost | More land, uneven distribution | Secondary |
| Distributed cathodic | Uniform distribution | Many installations | Consider if soil varies |

**Selection: DEEP ANODE GROUNDBED**
- Depth: 100-150 feet
- Number: 3-4 beds distributed along pipeline
- Each bed designed for 30-35 A nominal output

### Step 6: Anode Selection and Sizing

**Anode Material Selection:**

Types considered:
- **High-silicon cast iron (HSCI)** - Most common, good life
- **Graphite** - Lower consumption, higher initial cost
- **Mixed metal oxide (MMO)** - Longest life, high-tech

**Selection: HIGH-SILICON CAST IRON (HSCI)**
- ASTM A518 Grade 1 (14.5% Si)
- Consumption rate: 0.5-1.0 lb/A-year
- Typical weight per anode: 60 lbs

**Anode Quantity Calculation:**

Per Peabody's:
- Total current: 100 A
- Design life: 50 years
- Consumption: 0.75 lb/A-year (average)
- Total anode weight = 100 A × 50 years × 0.75 lb/A-year = 3,750 lbs

**Number of anodes:**
- 3,750 lbs ÷ 60 lbs/anode = 62.5 → **63 anodes**

**Distribution:**
- 4 deep groundbeds × 16 anodes each = 64 anodes

### Step 7: Rectifier Sizing

**Rectifier output requirements:**
- Current: 100 A nominal, 150 A maximum (50% capacity for future, aging)
- Voltage calculation: V = I × R

**Circuit resistance components:**
1. Anode-to-soil resistance (deep bed): ~0.5 Ω
2. Cable resistance: ~0.1 Ω
3. Pipeline-to-soil resistance: ~0.05 Ω

**Total circuit resistance: ~0.65 Ω**

**Rectifier voltage:**
- Operating voltage: 100 A × 0.65 Ω = 65 VDC
- Design voltage with margin: 80 VDC

**Rectifier specification:**
- Output: 150 A, 80 VDC
- AC input: 480V/3-phase (available)
- Efficiency: >85%
- Number required: 4 units (distributed)

### Step 8: Protection Criteria

Per NACE SP0169 Section 6:

**Criteria for adequate protection:**
- **Polarized potential**: -850 mV CSE (Copper-Copper Sulfate Electrode) or more negative
- **OR**: 100 mV polarization shift (from native potential)
- **OR**: -850 mV ON potential with IR drop consideration

**Design target:** -900 to -1,100 mV CSE

### Step 9: Monitoring Design

**Test stations required per NACE SP0169:**
- Every 1 mile minimum
- Additional at major crossings (road, rail, river)
- At insulating flange locations
- At CP station locations

**Test station design:**
- Pipe-to-soil potential measurement
- Current monitoring capability (shunts)
- Insulating flange monitoring

### Step 10: Expected Operating Parameters

| Parameter | Design Value | Range | Alarm Limits |
|-----------|--------------|-------|--------------|
| Rectifier output | 100 A | 80-120 A | <70 A, >140 A |
| Pipe potential | -950 mV CSE | -850 to -1,100 | >-800, <-1,200 |
| Current density | 3.0 mA/m² | 2.5-3.5 | >5.0 (coating damage) |
| Anode bed resistance | 0.5 Ω | 0.4-0.6 | >1.0 Ω |

## Summary Recommendations

1. **Install impressed current system** - 100 A capacity
2. **Deep anode groundbeds** - 4 locations, 16 HSCI anodes each
3. **Rectifiers** - 4 units, 150A/80V output
4. **Test stations** - 15 minimum locations
5. **Monitoring** - Remote monitoring at each rectifier

### References
- NACE SP0169-2013, Section 6 (Criteria)
- Peabody's Control of Pipeline Corrosion, Chapter 6
- NACE SP0169, Section 9 (Design)

Current density for FBE: 1-3 mA/m² selected

abnormal_variants.description: Low resistivity soil anomaly discovered - 500 ohm-cm section
abnormal_variants.modified_situation: Soil survey reveals 2-mile section with 500 ohm-cm resistivity (highly corrosive marshy area)
abnormal_variants.modified_expected_response: SOIL ANOMALY REQUIRES MODIFIED DESIGN. The 500 ohm-cm section is CRITICAL CORROSION AREA. Adjustments needed: (1) Increased current density to 10-15 mA/m² for affected section, (2) Additional groundbed near anomaly, (3) Enhanced coating inspection - holidays more likely, (4) Consider supplemental galvanic anodes for localized protection. Current requirement for 2-mile section: Area = 44,240 sq ft, Current = 44,240 × 10 mA/ft² = 442 A → Add dedicated 50 A rectifier. Total system now 150 A. Monitor closely - this section will be first failure point.

abnormal_variants.description: Adjacent pipeline with existing CP system presents interference
abnormal_variants.modified_situation: During design, discover parallel pipeline with impressed current CP creating -1,200 mV potential nearby
abnormal_variants.modified_expected_response: STRAY CURRENT INTERFERENCE ANALYSIS REQUIRED per NACE SP0169 Section 10. The existing CP may cause: (1) Current pickup on our pipeline (cathodic interference), (2) Over-protection in areas, (3) coating damage from hydrogen evolution. Actions: (1) Interference testing with current interrupters, (2) Coordination with adjacent operator on synchronized interruption, (3) Consider bonding to parallel line if mutually beneficial, (4) Adjust design for interference area. May need: additional test stations at crossing points, current drainage bonds, or joint coordination agreement with neighbor.

abnormal_variants.description: Access issue - property owner denies groundbed installation
abnormal_variants.modified_situation: Key groundbed location denied by landowner with legal standing. Alternatives are 3+ miles away
abnormal_variants.modified_expected_response: ACCESS ISSUE REQUIRES REDESIGN. Options: (1) Increase remaining groundbed capacity by 33% to cover gap (may cause over-protection in some areas), (2) Negotiate easement - offer compensation, (3) Use alternate technology: distributed anode system (more expensive), (4) Consider shallow horizontal beds in available areas. RECOMMEND combination: legal negotiation + distributed shallow beds as backup. Current attenuation over 3 miles will be significant - calculations show potential rise to -700 mV (under-protected). This is unacceptable. Must resolve access or redesign with distributed shallow beds every mile.

test_cases.name: Pipeline Wall Thickness Calculation
test_cases.situation: Design a new natural gas transmission pipeline for construction in 2026. The pipeline will transport processed natural gas from a processing plant to a distribution junction. Design parameters: Material: API 5L Grade X70 seamless pipe (SMYS = 70,000 psi per API 5L), Diameter: 42 inches NPS (42-inch nominal, 42.0-inch OD), Design pressure (MAOP): 1,440 psig, Class location: Class 2 (per ASME B31.8 - 11-45 dwellings per mile), Design temperature: 100°F (expected operating range 40°F-120°F), Corrosion allowance: 0.0625 inches (1/16 inch) for 50-year design life, Construction method: Traditional open-cut, Installation: Below ground with 36-inch minimum cover, Joint factor: 1.00 (seamless pipe longitudinal factor), Temperature derating: Required per B31.8 for temperatures above 100°F.
test_cases.task: Calculate the minimum required wall thickness per ASME B31.8 Section 841.1. Select appropriate commercial wall thickness and verify compliance.
test_cases.expected_response: Wall thickness calculation per ASME B31.8 Section 841.1.
test_cases.expected_response_detailed:
## Wall Thickness Calculation per ASME B31.8

### Step 1: Identify Design Formula

Per ASME B31.8-2022, Section 841.1.1, the wall thickness for steel pipe is determined by:

**t = (P × D) / (2 × S × F × E × T)**

Where:
- t = minimum wall thickness (inches)
- P = design pressure (psig) = 1,440 psi
- D = outside diameter (inches) = 42.0 inches
- S = specified minimum yield strength (SMYS) = 70,000 psi (X70)
- F = design factor (from Table 841.1.6-1) = 0.60 for Class 2
- E = longitudinal joint factor = 1.00 (seamless)
- T = temperature derating factor = 1.000 (for 100°F, per Table 841.1.6-2)

### Step 2: Determine Design Parameters

**Design Factor (F) Selection:**

Per ASME B31.8 Table 841.1.6-1:

| Class Location | Design Factor F |
|----------------|----------------|
| Class 1 (rural/sparsely populated) | 0.80 |
| Class 2 (11-45 dwellings/mile) | 0.60 |
| Class 3 (46+ dwellings/mile) | 0.50 |
| Class 4 (multi-story buildings) | 0.40 |

**Selected: F = 0.60** (Class 2 confirmed)

**Temperature Derating Factor (T):**

Per ASME B31.8 Table 841.1.6-2:

| Temperature | Derating Factor T |
|-------------|------------------|
| ≤250°F | 1.000 |
| 300°F | 0.967 |
| 350°F | 0.933 |
| 400°F | 0.900 |

**Selected: T = 1.000** (100°F is below 250°F)

**Longitudinal Joint Factor (E):**

Per ASME B31.8 Table 841.1.6-3:
- Seamless pipe: E = 1.00
- Electric resistance welded: E = 1.00
- Submerged arc welded: E = 1.00

**Selected: E = 1.00** (seamless)

### Step 3: Calculate Minimum Wall Thickness

**Formula:**
t = (P × D) / (2 × S × F × E × T)

**Substitute values:**
t = (1,440 psi × 42.0 in) / (2 × 70,000 psi × 0.60 × 1.00 × 1.00)

**Numerator:**
1,440 × 42.0 = 60,480 psi-in

**Denominator:**
2 × 70,000 × 0.60 × 1.00 × 1.00 = 84,000

**Calculation:**
t = 60,480 / 84,000
t = 0.720 inches

**Minimum required wall thickness: 0.720 inches**

### Step 4: Apply Corrosion Allowance

Total required thickness = Calculated thickness + Corrosion allowance
Total required = 0.720 + 0.0625 = **0.7825 inches**

### Step 5: Select Commercial Wall Thickness

API 5L specifies standard wall thicknesses. For 42" pipe:

| Nominal Wall | Thickness (in) | Weight (lb/ft) |
|--------------|----------------|----------------|
| Std | 0.500 | 222.0 |
| X-Strong | 0.625 | 276.0 |
| - | 0.750 | 329.0 |
| - | 0.875 | 382.0 |
| - | 1.000 | 434.0 |

**Selection: 0.750 inches**

Rationale:
- 0.750 inches > 0.7825 inches required (ACCEPTABLE)
- Next standard down (0.625) is insufficient
- 0.750 provides 0.9375 inches excess (safety margin)
- Weight: 329 lb/ft (manageable for construction)

### Step 6: Verify Hoop Stress

Verify actual hoop stress is within limits:

**Hoop stress formula:**
S_h = (P × D) / (2 × t)

**Calculate:**
S_h = (1,440 × 42.0) / (2 × 0.750)
S_h = 60,480 / 1.50
S_h = 40,320 psi

**Allowable stress:**
S_allowable = S × F × E × T
S_allowable = 70,000 × 0.60 × 1.00 × 1.00
S_allowable = 42,000 psi

**Verification:**
Actual (40,320 psi) < Allowable (42,000 psi) ✓

% of SMYS = 40,320 / 70,000 = 57.6%

This is well within limits.

### Step 7: Check Other Design Considerations

**Minimum Wall Thickness per B31.8:**
- No additional minimum specified for 42" beyond calculated

**Internal pressure vs external loading:**
- Wall thickness adequate for 1,440 psig internal pressure
- 0.750 inches provides adequate stiffness for handling/construction

**Temperature effects:**
- Design temperature 100°F is within normal range
- No additional derating needed

**Future considerations:**
- Corrosion allowance of 0.0625 inches accommodates 50-year degradation
- Pressure derating over time can be accommodated

## Summary and Recommendations

| Parameter | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Calculated minimum | 0.720 inches |
| With corrosion allowance | 0.7825 inches |
| Selected commercial wall | 0.750 inches |
| | |
| Design pressure | 1,440 psig |
| Actual hoop stress | 40,320 psi |
| Allowable stress | 42,000 psi |
| % of SMYS | 57.6% |

### Final Answer

**Minimum required wall thickness: 0.7825 inches (including corrosion allowance)**

**Recommended commercial wall thickness: 0.750 inches API 5L X70 seamless**

**Verification: 0.750 > 0.7825 required - ACCEPTABLE**

Wait - correction needed:

**Re-evaluation:** 0.750 inches is LESS than required 0.7825 inches. This is NOT acceptable.

**Revised selection: 0.875 inches**

- 0.875 > 0.7825 ✓
- Provides additional safety margin
- Weight: 382 lb/ft

### References
- ASME B31.8-2022, Section 841.1.1 (Design formula)
- ASME B31.8 Table 841.1.6-1 (Design factors)
- ASME B31.8 Table 841.1.6-2 (Temperature factors)
- API 5L (Pipe specifications)
expected_elements: P = 1,440 psi, D = 42.0 in, S = 70,000 psi identified

formula_application.description: Correct B31.8 formula and all variables identified

design_factor.description: F = 0.60 for Class 2 with table reference

calculation.description: Minimum thickness calculation (0.720 in) and with corrosion (0.7825 in)

commercial_selection.description: Proper commercial wall selection (0.875 in, recognizing 0.750 insufficient)

verification.description: Hoop stress check and acceptance confirmation

abnormal_variants.description: Class location change from 2 to 3 during construction
abnormal_variants.modified_situation: Housing development approved - class location changes to Class 3 (46+ dwellings/mile) after pipe ordered
abnormal_variants.modified_expected_response: CLASS CHANGE REQUIRES REDESIGN. Per B31.8 Table 841.1.6-1: Class 3 F = 0.50 (vs 0.60 Class 2). Recalculate: t = (1,440 × 42) / (2 × 70,000 × 0.50 × 1 × 1) = 60,480 / 70,000 = 0.864 in. With corrosion: 0.9265 in. REQUIRED: 1.0 inch commercial wall. Current 0.750 or 0.875 pipe is INSUFFICIENT. Options: (1) Reorder 1.0 inch wall (schedule/cost impact), (2) Reduce MAOP to 1,200 psig (recalculate F=0.5, P=1,200 → t=0.72+0.0625=0.7825, 0.875 acceptable), (3) Request variance (unlikely). RECOMMEND: Pressure reduction to 1,200 psig approved by stakeholders.

abnormal_variants.description: Temperature increase to 180°F for compressor discharge
abnormal_variants.modified_situation: Design changed - section near compressor discharge at 180°F
abnormal_variants.modified_expected_response: HIGH TEMPERATURE DERATING REQUIRED. Per B31.8 Table 841.1.6-2: At 180°F, T = 1.000 (≤250°F threshold). No derating required for 180°F. HOWEVER: verify pipe material specs - some X70 grades have temperature limitations. Also consider: thermal expansion (expansion loops), insulation at supports, bellows or expansion joints. Temperature cycling considerations for fatigue. The T factor remains 1.0, but thermal stress analysis is now required per B31.8 Section 833. Continue with original design.

abnormal_variants.description: ERW pipe substituted for seamless due to supply shortage
abnormal_variants.modified_situation: Supply chain issues - seamless not available, ERW proposed
abnormal_variants.modified_expected_response: ERW SUBSTITUTION ALLOWABLE with considerations. Per B31.8 Table 841.1.6-3: ERW has E = 1.00 (same as seamless for hoop stress). BUT critical differences: (1) ERW has lower toughness, verify notch toughness requirements per B31.8 Section 814, (2) ERW has weld seam - inspect per API 5L, (3) Cold expansion limitations for ERW in high-pressure service, (4) Some companies restrict ERW above certain pressures. RECOMMEND: Review company standards, require additional radiographic inspection of ERW seams, consider pressure limitation. Formula calculation remains same (E=1.0). Ensure ERW meets API 5L PSL2 requirements.

test_cases.name: Gas Compressibility Factor Calculation
test_cases.situation: Calculate gas properties for pipeline hydraulic analysis. The pipeline transports natural gas from a remote production facility to a processing plant. The gas composition has been analyzed and found suitable for pipeline transport. Pipeline specifications: Gas composition: 95% methane, 3% ethane, 2% propane (by mole), Specific gravity: 0.60 (relative to air = 1.0), Higher heating value: 1,050 Btu/scf, Operating pressure: 1,000 psig (stage 1 compression), Operating temperature: 80°F average, Pipeline: 24-inch NPS, 50 miles length, Elevation profile: generally flat (±50 feet), Base conditions: 60°F, 14.696 psia. Design considerations: Need actual volumetric flow rate at operating conditions for compressor sizing, Standard volume needed for custody transfer measurement, Pressure drop calculation requires accurate Z factor, Project requires ±2% accuracy on flow calculations.
test_cases.task: Calculate the compressibility factor (Z) using AGA Report No. 8 (detailed method), and determine the actual gas volume at operating conditions for a given standard volume of 100 MMSCFD. Show all calculations and verify accuracy suitability.
test_cases.expected_response: Compressibility factor calculation per AGA Report No. 8 methodology.
test_cases.expected_response_detailed:
## Compressibility Factor Calculation per AGA Report No. 8

### Step 1: Define Parameters and Convert Units

**Given Data:**
- Composition: 95% C1, 3% C2, 2% C3
- Specific gravity: 0.60
- Operating pressure: 1,000 psig = 1,014.7 psia
- Operating temperature: 80°F = 539.67°R (Rankine)
- Base pressure: 14.696 psia
- Base temperature: 60°F = 519.67°R
- Gas constant R: 10.7316 (psia·ft³)/(lb-mol·°R)

**Unit Conversions:**
- 80°F + 459.67 = 539.67°R
- 1,000 psig + 14.7 = 1,014.7 psia

### Step 2: Calculate Pseudo-Critical Properties

Per AGA Report No. 8 and gas component tables:

**Critical Properties from Component Data:**

| Component | Mole % | Tc (°R) | Pc (psia) | yi×Tc | yi×Pc |
|-----------|--------|---------|-----------|-------|-------|
| Methane (C1) | 0.95 | 343.33 | 673.1 | 326.16 | 639.44 |
| Ethane (C2) | 0.03 | 549.92 | 708.3 | 16.50 | 21.25 |
| Propane (C3) | 0.02 | 666.06 | 617.4 | 13.32 | 12.35 |
| **TOTALS** | 1.00 | - | - | **355.98** | **673.04** |

**Pseudo-critical temperature:**
Tpc = Σ(yi × Tci) = 355.98°R ≈ **356.0°R**

**Pseudo-critical pressure:**
Ppc = Σ(yi × Pci) = 673.04 psia ≈ **673.0 psia**

### Step 3: Calculate Reduced Properties

**Pseudo-reduced temperature:**
Tr = T / Tpc = 539.67 / 356.0 = 1.516

**Pseudo-reduced pressure:**
Pr = P / Ppc = 1,014.7 / 673.0 = 1.508

### Step 4: Determine Compressibility Factor using AGA-8

**AGA Report No. 8 Method:**

The detailed method uses 21 equations with gas composition. For hand calculation, we use the Standing-Katz correlation (simplified AGA-8 approach).

**Generalized compressibility equation:**
Z = f(Tr, Pr)

Using Standing-Katz charts or polynomial:

For Tr = 1.516 and Pr = 1.508:

From Standing-Katz correlation (or iterative calculation):

The compressibility factor Z is determined from reduced properties:

At Tr = 1.52, Pr = 1.51:

From correlation tables: **Z ≈ 0.855**

**Alternative: AGA-8 NX-19 Calculation**

For verification using NX-19 method:

B = Tr = 1.516
A = Pr = 1.508

Using AGA-8 subroutines (accurate for sweet natural gas):

**Calculated Z = 0.852** (using detailed method)

**Selected: Z = 0.85** (conservative rounding)

### Step 5: Calculate Actual Gas Volume

Given: Standard volume = 100 MMSCFD = 100,000,000 SCF/day

**Real Gas Law:**
PV = ZnRT

**Conversion from standard to actual:**

Q_actual = Q_standard × (P_base/P) × (T/T_base) × (1/Z)

**Where:**
- Q_standard = 100 MMSCFD = 1.157 MMSCF (per second - convert for actual)
- Better: work in consistent units

**Calculation:**
Q_actual = 100 MMSCFD × (14.696/1,014.7) × (539.67/519.67) × (1/0.85)

**Step by step:**
1. Pressure ratio: 14.696 / 1,014.7 = 0.01448
2. Temperature ratio: 539.67 / 519.67 = 1.0385
3. Z factor: 1 / 0.85 = 1.176
4. Combined: 0.01448 × 1.0385 × 1.176 = 0.01768

**Q_actual = 100 × 0.01768 = 1.768 MMSCFD at actual conditions**

Wait - this seems low. Let's recalculate:

Actual volume should be SMALLER than standard due to compression.

**Correct calculation:**
Q_actual = Q_standard × (P_base/P) × (T/T_base) × Z

Q_actual = 100 × (14.696/1,014.7) × (539.67/519.67) × 0.85

**Step by step:**
1. 14.696 / 1,014.7 = 0.01448
2. 539.67 / 519.67 = 1.0385
3. 0.01448 × 1.0385 = 0.01504
4. 0.01504 × 0.85 = 0.01278
5. 100 × 0.01278 = **1.278 MMSCFD**

This seems more reasonable - compressed gas occupies less volume.

But wait - verify direction:
- At 1,000 psig, gas is compressed → should have smaller volume
- Conversion factor < 1 is expected

**Actual volumetric flow rate: 1.278 MMSCFD**

Convert to ACFM (actual cubic feet per minute):
1.278 × 10⁶ SCF/day ÷ 1440 min/day = 887 ACFM

### Step 6: Calculate Gas Density

**Molar mass calculation:**
- Methane (0.95): 16.04 g/mol × 0.95 = 15.24
- Ethane (0.03): 30.07 g/mol × 0.03 = 0.90
- Propane (0.02): 44.10 g/mol × 0.02 = 0.88
- **Molar mass: ~17.02 g/mol**

**Molar mass of air: 28.97 g/mol**
**Specific gravity: 17.02/28.97 = 0.588 ≈ 0.60 ✓**

**Ideal gas density at standard:**
ρ_std = (P × M) / (R × T)

**Actual density:**
ρ_actual = ρ_std × (P/P_base) × (T_base/T) × (1/Z)

### Step 7: Verification and Accuracy Assessment

**Accuracy verification:**

| Method | Z Value | Notes |
|--------|---------|-------|
| Standing-Katz | 0.855 | Chart correlation |
| AGA-8 Detailed | 0.852 | 21 equation method |
| AGA-8 NX-19 | 0.853 | Simplified |
| Selected | 0.85 | Conservative |

**Accuracy: ±0.5%** which is well within project ±2% requirement.

**Range validation:**
- Tr = 1.52 is well above critical point crossover
- Pr = 1.51 is in range where AGA-8 is highly accurate
- Natural gas mixture is within AGA-8 applicability (sweet gas, no H2S)

## Summary

| Parameter | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Gas composition | 95% C1, 3% C2, 2% C3 |
| Specific gravity | 0.60 |
| Operating conditions | 1,000 psig, 80°F |
| Pseudo-critical T | 356.0°R |
| Pseudo-critical P | 673.0 psia |
| Reduced T | 1.516 |
| Reduced P | 1.508 |
| **Compressibility factor Z** | **0.85** |
| Standard flow | 100 MMSCFD |
| **Actual flow** | **1.278 MMSCFD** |
| ACFM | 887 ACFM |

### References
- AGA Report No. 8: Compressibility and Supercompressibility for Natural Gas
- Standing-Katz Compressibility Correlation
- Engineering Data Book, GPSA
expected_elements: Unit conversions: °F to °R (+459.67), psig to psia (+14.7)

pseudo_critical_calculation.description: Correct Tpc (356°R) and Ppc (673 psia) calculations

reduced_properties.description: Tr (1.516) and Pr (1.508) calculations with correct formulas

z_factor.description: Z = 0.85 from AGA-8 with reference to method

volume_conversion.description: Actual volume 1.278 MMSCFD with correct real gas law application

verification_accuracy.description: Verification methods and accuracy assessment against 2% requirement

abnormal_variants.description: Sour gas with 5% CO2 and 50 ppm H2S
abnormal_variants.modified_situation: Gas analysis reveals CO2 and H2S content - sour gas conditions
abnormal_variants.modified_expected_response: SOUR GAS - AGA-8 VERIFICATION REQUIRED. AGA Report No. 8 is designed for sweet natural gas. With H2S present: (1) Acid gas corrections may be needed per AGA-8 Section 4.3, (2) Consider AGA-8 with acid gas subroutines, (3) Check if within AGA-8 validity range (<15% CO2, <5% H2S total acid gas). At 5% CO2 + 50 ppm H2S, still within sweet gas definition (<4 ppm H2S = sour by some definitions, but 50 ppm is definitely sour). RECOMMEND: Use AGA-8 with caution, or consider alternative methods (SRK, PR EOS) for comparison. The presence of polar molecules (H2S) can affect Z factor. Conservatively, add 1-2% to Z for sour gas effects: Z ≈ 0.87. Monitor for hydrates at 1,000 psig.

abnormal_variants.modified_situation: Compressor discharge pressure increased to 2,500 psig
abnormal_variants.modified_expected_response: HIGH PRESSURE - EXTENDED AGA-8 REQUIRED. At 2,500 psig: Pr = 2,514.7/673 = 3.74. Tr = 1.516 (same). At Pr = 3.74, we are at HIGH REDUCED PRESSURE. AGA-8 is valid up to Pr ≈ 15, but accuracy decreases. At these conditions: (1) Gas behaves less ideally, Z will be LOWER (more compressible at high P), (2) Estimated Z ≈ 0.75-0.78, (3) Use AGA-8 detailed method, (4) Consider laboratory measurement for critical applications. Recalculate: Q_actual = 100 × (14.696/2514.7) × (539.67/519.67) × 0.76 = 100 × 0.00584 × 1.0385 × 0.76 = 0.461 MMSCFD. Volume reduction is more dramatic at higher pressure.

abnormal_variants.modified_situation: Winter operation drops to 20°F at ground level
abnormal_variants.modified_expected_response: LOW TEMPERATURE - HYDRATE WARNING. At 20°F + 459.67 = 479.67°R. Tr = 479.67/356 = 1.35 (still above critical). Recalculate Z: At lower T, Z typically DECREASES slightly. Tr = 1.35, Pr = 1.51 → Z ≈ 0.81 (vs 0.85 at 80°F). More important: HYDRATE FORMATION RISK. Natural gas with 0.6 SG forms hydrates around 50-60°F at 1,000 psig. At 20°F, hydrates are GUARANTEED without inhibitor. RECOMMEND: (1) Add methanol or glycol injection, (2) Line heating, or (3) Insulation. The Z calculation is secondary to the operational hazard. Recalculate flow: 100 × 0.01448 × (479.67/519.67) × 0.81 = 100 × 0.01448 × 0.923 × 0.81 = 1.083 MMSCFD. Lower temperature reduces volume further.

test_cases.name: Control Valve Sizing for Compressor Station
test_cases.situation: A new compressor station requires a control valve to regulate gas flow from second stage compression discharge to transmission pipeline. Operating conditions: Inlet pressure (P1): 1,200 psig, Outlet pressure (P2): 800 psig, Design flow: 350 MMSCFD, Maximum flow: 425 MMSCFD, Minimum flow: 100 MMSCFD. Gas specific gravity: 0.60, Temperature: 100°F, Z: 0.85, Pc: 673 psia, Tc: 356°R. Required turndown: 4:1. Valve: Globe with linear characteristic. Line size: 24-inch. Noise limit: 85 dBA.
test_cases.task: Size the control valve using ISA-75.01: calculate required Cv for design, maximum, and minimum conditions, determine valve size (typically 1-2 line sizes smaller), calculate actual Cv at all conditions, calculate percent opening at design flow, evaluate noise level using IEC 60534-8-3, check for cavitation, verify operating range (15-80% opening preferred). Specify body size, trim size, and rated Cv.
test_cases.expected_response_detailed:
## Control Valve Sizing per ISA-75.01

### Parameters
- P1 = 1,214.7 psia, P2 = 814.7 psia, ΔP = 400 psid
- T = 559.67°R, Q = 350 MMSCFD, G = 0.60, Z = 0.85

### Critical Flow Check
- P2/P1 = 814.7/1214.7 = 0.671
- Fk × χ = 0.907 × 0.65 = 0.590
- 0.671 > 0.590: NOT choked flow

### Cv Calculations
- Expansion factor Y = 0.696
- Cv_design = 8,661
- Cv_max = 10,515
- Cv_min = 2,477

### Valve Selection
- Line size: 24 inches
- Selected: 20-inch ANSI 600 globe valve
- Trim: 16-inch, Rated Cv: 15,000

### Travel Calculations
- Design: 8,661/15,000 = 57.8%
- Maximum: 10,515/15,000 = 70.1%
- Minimum: 2,477/15,000 = 16.5%
- All within 15-80% acceptable range ✓

### Noise
- Estimated: 85-90 dBA
- Specify low-noise trim

### References
- ISA-75.01.01
- IEC 60534-8-3

abnormal_variants.description: Choked flow condition detected at maximum flow
abnormal_variants.modified_expected_response: CHOKED FLOW - OVERSIZE REQUIRED. If choked, need larger valve or different trim. Consider characterized cage or multi-stage trim to handle high pressure drop without choking.

test_cases.name: Pipeline Stress Analysis - River Crossing
test_cases.situation: A 36-inch pipeline crosses a river through a horizontal directional drill (HDD). The crossing is 1,800 feet long with the following profile: Entry point at elevation 850 feet, exit point at elevation 855 feet, river bottom at deepest point elevation 815 feet located at 900 feet along profile. The pipeline is API 5L X65 with 0.625-inch wall thickness. Operating pressure is 1,200 psig. Soil conditions: Entry/exit zones sandy clay, river bottom loose silt. Groundwater table is at 840 feet elevation. Winter temperature: -10°F, summer: 95°F. Construction: Pullback completed, pipeline empty during installation, hydrotest at 1,500 psig.
test_cases.task: Perform stress analysis per ASME B31.8 and API RP 1111. Calculate bending stresses from HDD profile, determine if stress ratios are within limits, evaluate hydrotest stresses, check for upheaval buckling potential, and specify required depth of cover at river bottom.
test_cases.expected_response_detailed:
## Stress Analysis per ASME B31.8 and API RP 1111

### Pipeline Parameters
- Diameter: 36 inches, Wall: 0.625 inches
- Material: API 5L X65 (SMYS = 65,000 psi)
- Maximum profile depth: 35 feet below entry

### Bending Stress Calculation

**HDD Bending Radius:**
- Minimum radius per API RP 1111: 1,200 × D = 1,200 × 36 = 1200 inches = 100 feet
- Actual radius at deepest point: ~1,200 feet (from profile)
- Much greater than minimum - ACCEPTABLE

**Bending Stress Formula:**
σ_bending = (E × D) / (2 × R)
Where E = 29×10⁶ psi, D = 36 in, R = 1,200×12 = 14,400 in

σ_bending = (29×10⁶ × 36) / (2 × 14,400) = 36,250 psi

**Total Stress:**
- Hoop: PD/2t = (1,200×36)/(2×0.625) = 34,560 psi
- Axial: Pr/2t = 34,560/2 = 17,280 psi
- Combined: Using von Mises or Tresca

**ASME B31.8 Limits:**
- Design factor F = 0.72 (Class 1) = 46,800 psi
- Combined stress <90% SMYS = 58,500 psi

### Hydrotest Stress
- P_test = 1,500 psig
- Hoop = (1,500×36)/(2×0.625) = 43,200 psi
- Within 90% SMYS (58,500 psi) - ACCEPTABLE

### Depth of Cover
- API RP 1111: Minimum 10 feet at river bottom
- Actual: Entry 850 ft - 815 ft = 35 feet
- Well above minimum - ACCEPTABLE

### References
- ASME B31.8 Section 833
- API RP 1111 Section 4

abnormal_variants.modified_expected_response: OVER-STRESS CONDITION. If bending stress exceeds limits: increase radius, reduce entry angle, or increase wall thickness. May require re-design of HDD profile.

test_cases.name: Wall Thickness Design - Class Location
test_cases.situation: Design a new natural gas pipeline: Material: API 5L Grade X70 (SMYS 70,000 psi), Diameter: 42 inches NPS (42-inch OD), Design pressure (MAOP): 1,440 psig, Class location: Class 2 per ASME B31.8, Design temperature: 100°F, Corrosion allowance: 0.0625 inches, Construction: Traditional open-cut, Below ground with 36-inch minimum cover.
test_cases.task: Calculate minimum required wall thickness per ASME B31.8 Section 841.1. Select appropriate commercial wall thickness and verify compliance. Verify hoop stress is within allowable limits.
test_cases.expected_response_detailed:
## Wall Thickness Calculation per ASME B31.8

### Design Formula
t = (P × D) / (2 × S × F × E × T)

### Parameters
- P = 1,440 psi
- D = 42.0 inches
- S = 70,000 psi (X70 SMYS)
- F = 0.60 (Class 2 from Table 841.1.6-1)
- E = 1.00 (seamless)
- T = 1.000 (100°F ≤ 250°F)

### Calculation
t = (1,440 × 42) / (2 × 70,000 × 0.60 × 1.00 × 1.00)
t = 60,480 / 84,000
t = 0.720 inches

### Corrosion Allowance
Total = 0.720 + 0.0625 = 0.7825 inches

### Commercial Selection
- 0.750 inches: Insufficient (0.750 < 0.7825)
- 0.875 inches: ACCEPTABLE (0.875 > 0.7825)
- Selection: 0.875 inches

### Hoop Stress Verification
σ_h = (P × D) / (2 × t) = (1,440 × 42) / (2 × 0.875) = 40,320 psi
σ_allowable = S × F × E × T = 70,000 × 0.60 = 42,000 psi
40,320 < 42,000 ✓ ACCEPTABLE

### References
- ASME B31.8-2022 Section 841.1.1
- Table 841.1.6-1 (Design factors)

abnormal_variants.description: Class location changes to Class 3 after pipe ordered
abnormal_variants.modified_expected_response: CLASS 3 REQUIRES REDESIGN. F = 0.50 for Class 3. Recalculate: t = 0.864 in, required 0.9265 in. Current 0.875 pipe INSUFFICIENT. Options: reduce MAOP to 1,200 psig or reorder 1.0 inch wall.

test_cases.name: Pipeline Anchor Design
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: River crossing anchors: thrust = pressure × area = 1,200 psig × 28.27 in² = 33,924 lbs. Safety factor 2.5: anchor capacity 84,810 lbs. Concrete anchor 5,000 lbs/yd³: volume needed 17 cubic yards. Include thermal movement accommodation, riverbed scour protection, cathodic isolation if dissimilar metals, 2-foot burial below scour depth per B31.8.
test_cases.task: Provide detailed analysis per applicable standards and codes.

test_cases.name: Compressor Station Layout
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: NFPA 37 spacing: 50 feet from property line, 10 feet between compressors (with 1-hour rated wall), 75 feet from high occupancy, noise 55 dBA at property line per OSHA 1910.95. Ventilation: 6 air changes/hour minimum, LEL detection at low points, fire-rated doors (1.5 hour), emergency shutdown within 100 feet of any point, eye wash/safety shower for chemical area, deluge system for compressors per NFPA 37.
test_cases.task: Provide detailed analysis per applicable standards and codes.

test_cases.name: Flow Equation Selection
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: AGA NX-19 for high pressure transmission (typical for pipelines >500 psig and >12 inches). Weymouth for early planning/quick estimates. Panhandle A for longer lines with Reynolds >4,000. Modified Panhandle for higher accuracy with elevation changes. Isothermal flow assumption valid when temperature variation <20°F. B31.8 non-isothermal correction for significant elevation change.
test_cases.task: Provide detailed analysis per applicable standards and codes.

test_cases.name: Valve Actuation Time
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: API 6D for pipeline valves: closure time target <30 seconds for 16-inch. Hydraulic actuator sizing: torque = 2,500 ft-lb for double-block valve. Operating pressure 1,500 psig, accumulator 2,500 psi, cylinder 6-inch bore. Valve CV requirement for blowdown: calculate orifice per API 521. Manual override required for power loss. Position indication to SCADA required for Class 3/4.
test_cases.task: Provide detailed analysis per applicable standards and codes.

test_cases.name: Pig Launcher Design
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: Barrel diameter: 1.25 × pipe ID (30-inch becomes 37.5-inch). Length: 2× longest pig + 10 feet typically. Pressure rating: MAOP + test margin. Closure: quick-opening closure rated for cycles, safety interlock. Drain: 2-inch minimum to containment. Vent: 4-inch to safe elevation or flare. Bypass line for equalization. Supports: saddle supports with slide plates for thermal movement. ASME B31.3 for launcher piping.
test_cases.task: Provide detailed analysis per applicable standards and codes.

test_cases.name: Scraper Design for Paraffin Removal
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: Progressive cavity pump requires: bypass around pump for pigging (3-port valve), scraper selection based on deposit thickness (mandrel pigs for light deposits, brush pigs for heavy), by-pass holes in discs for wax removal, speed 2-5 ft/sec optimum, tracking required for paraffin monitoring, batch vs continuous treatment evaluation, chemical inhibition if recurring, production impact during pigging, receiver capacity for debris.
test_cases.task: Provide detailed analysis per applicable standards and codes.

test_cases.name: Vibration Analysis - Reciprocating Compressor
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: Pulsation study per API 618: pulsation bottle sizing for 1-2% peak-to-peak pressure variation. Acoustic filter: Helmholtz resonator if 2× frequency matches mechanical natural frequency. Mechanical vibration: 0.15 inches/second RMS maximum at bearings. Piping: 5× diameter minimum from bend to compressor. Orifice plates for pulsation damping. Study covers 0-2× running speed. Reciprocating: torque fluctuation analysis. Foundation: dynamic stiffness for vibration isolation.
test_cases.task: Provide detailed analysis per applicable standards and codes.

test_cases.name: Fugitive Emissions Calculation
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: EPA Method 21: screening values per leak definition. LDAR program: 500 ppm threshold for first attempt, repair within 15 days, re-screen within 90. Component counts: valves, flanges, pump seals, compressor seals. Emission factors: valves 0.0056 kg/hr (gas), flanges 0.0006 kg/hr. Annual throughput 250 MMSCFD = 365 days × leak factor. Greenhouse gas: methane 25× CO2. Reporting required >25,000 metric tons CO2e annually for GHGRP.
test_cases.task: Provide detailed analysis per applicable standards and codes.

test_cases.name: Pipeline Crossings - Railroad
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: DOT crossing regulations: 6-foot minimum cover under track (reinforced concrete if less). Railroad approval required: design submission 180 days in advance. Cathodic protection isolation at crossing. Casings: carrier pipe inside casing with casing insulators (electrical isolation vent to atmosphere). Bored crossings: HDD minimum 10-foot depth below subgrade. Monitoring: settlement gauges during construction. Insurance: railroad protective liability required.
test_cases.task: Provide detailed analysis per applicable standards and codes.

test_cases.name: Meter Station Uncertainty
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: Orifice meter uncertainty: 0.5% of reading (AGA Report 3). Ultrasonic: 0.2% if calibrated, 0.5% if not. Coriolis: 0.1% for mass flow. Temperature correction: ±2°F = ±0.4% uncertainty. Pressure: ±2 psi at 1,000 psi = ±0.2%. Combined uncertainty: square root of sum of squares. Check: 5-year recalibration per API 21.1. Station balance: ±0.2% shipper/receiver agreement.
test_cases.task: Provide detailed analysis per applicable standards and codes.

test_cases.name: Fiber Optic Cable Installation
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: Cable in conduit: HDPE pipe 1.25-inch inside 2-inch conduit minimum, 40-foot spacing of intermediate access points, pulling tension 600 lbs maximum, bend radius 10× cable diameter, splice closures every 2 miles, SCADA integration for leak detection and third-party interference detection, power at each station for amplifiers, redundancy for critical sections (diverse routing if available), OTDR testing after installation for attenuation verification.
test_cases.task: Provide detailed analysis per applicable standards and codes.

test_cases.name: Buckle Arrestor Spacing
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: Offshore pipeline: buckle arrestor spacing based on propagation pressure. Propagation pressure 2,500 psi for 24-inch X65. Arrestor required within 1,000 feet of potential buckle sources (fishing gear, anchors). Design: concrete or steel sleeves. Spacing formula: P_prop/γ_water - external pressure margin. Limit state design: 1.1 safety factor on propagation. Installation: over pipe without girth weld interference, cathodic protection continuity or isolation depending on design.
test_cases.task: Provide detailed analysis per applicable standards and codes.

test_cases.name: Slug Catcher Sizing
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: Slug catcher vessel: inlet separator for two-phase flow. Volume based on maximum expected slug from pigging operation. 1,000 barrels liquid holdup for offshore, 2-4 hours residence time onshore. Diameter selected for vapor velocity (0.5-1.5 ft/sec). Liquid level control: 30-60% operating range. Instrumentation: level transmitter, pressure safety relief, temperature, densitometer for liquid composition. Per API 14E.
test_cases.task: Provide detailed analysis per applicable standards and codes.

test_cases.name: Pipe Support Spacing Calculation
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: API 570 spacing: span = √(0.33 × E × I / w) where E modulus, I moment of inertia, w distributed load. 24-inch schedule 40: span approximately 25 feet for liquid service, 35 feet gas. Account for concentrated loads (valves). Allowable deflection 0.5 inch. Thermal expansion guide spacing: full guide every second support. Shoe height for insulation maintenance.
test_cases.task: Provide detailed analysis per applicable standards and codes.

test_cases.name: Strain Based Design Limitations
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: SBD for extreme terrain: tensile strain limit 0.5% typical, 2.5% with deformation control. Compressive strain: 0.2% wrinkle initiation, 0.5-1% acceptance with wrinkles monitored. Notched bar compression test for girth weld. Bending strain monitoring during installation. Ovality limit 3% per API 5L. Crack arrest required if defect tolerance lower than flaw size.
test_cases.task: Provide detailed analysis per applicable standards and codes.

test_cases.name: Gas Dehydration Unit Design
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: Glycol dehydration: TEG circulation rate 2-5 gallons per lb water removed. Reboiler 400°F max (TEG degradation). Stripping gas if <7 lb/MMscf specification. Regenerator tray efficiency. Flash tank 75 psig minimum, 15-minute retention. Filter for hydrocarbon removal. pH adjustment for corrosion. Glycol losses 0.05-0.1 gallon/MMscf.
test_cases.task: Provide detailed analysis per applicable standards and codes.

test_cases.name: Pipeline Surge Analysis
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: Water hammer for liquid pipelines: Joukowsky equation ΔP = ρ × a × ΔV. Wave speed a = √(1/ρ × (1/K + D/(t×E))). Surge pressure can exceed 2× operating. Mitigation: surge tanks, controlled valve closure (10+ seconds), check valves with dashpots, pressure relief. Transient software (OLGA, PipelineStudio) for modeling. Worst case: ESD instantaneous closure.
test_cases.task: Provide detailed analysis per applicable standards and codes.

test_cases.name: Cathodic Protection Rectifier Sizing
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: Rectifier current output: based on coating quality and surface area. New FBE coating: 0.1-0.5 mA/sq ft. Poor coating: 2-10 mA/sq ft. Voltage output: overcome soil resistivity and anode bed resistance. Typical pipe ground bed: 50-amp output, 24 volts. Deep anode beds for congested areas. Remote monitoring via SCADA. Transformer-rectifier efficiency 70%. Reference electrode calibration annually per NACE SP0169.
test_cases.task: Provide detailed analysis per applicable standards and codes.

test_cases.name: Block Valve Spacing per Class
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: 192.745 spacing: Class 1 (rural) 10 miles, Class 2 (suburban) 7.5 miles, Class 3 (towns) 4 miles, Class 4 (urban) 2.5 miles. Exceptions: navigable waterways every crossing, HCA within limits, offshore platforms. Automatic or remote-controlled for Class 3/4. Manual acceptable Class 1/2 if accessible within 1 hour. Test frequency: partial stroke monthly, full stroke annually. Location markers required.
test_cases.task: Provide detailed analysis per applicable standards and codes.

test_cases.name: Gas Quality Specification Limits
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: Pipeline tariff specifications: H2S <0.25-1 grain/100 scf (4-16 ppm), CO2 <2-3%, moisture dew point 20°F below coldest operating, heating value 950-1,150 Btu/scf, hydrocarbon dew point line conditions (no liquids), O2 <10 ppm, total sulfur <5-20 grains. Wobbe index for interchangeability. Delivery specifications in interconnect agreements per GPA 2261.
test_cases.task: Provide detailed analysis per applicable standards and codes.

test_cases.name: Pipeline Re-Rating Procedure
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: Re-rating per 192.619: if test data available new MAOP = test pressure / 1.25. If older than 5 years: 85% of test or 80% of MAOP if never pressure tested. Engineering analysis for wall thickness, material properties, elevation profile, class location changes. Pressure reduction notification to PHMSA within 5 days. Customer notification if contractual MAOP affected. Records updated to reflect new MAOP.
test_cases.task: Provide detailed analysis per applicable standards and codes.

test_cases.name: Pipeline Upheaval Buckling
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: Upheaval in buried pipelines: thermal expansion with vertical restraint creates compressive axial load. Critical condition when axial force exceeds >4EI/L² (Euler buckling). Prevention: deeper burial, trench backfill quality, intermittent rock dump (virtual anchor), vertical loops for expansion, on-bottom stability for offshore. Design: Hobbs upheaval formula, vertical out-of-straightness limit 0.5% typically, effective axial force calculation including temperature and pressure effects.
test_cases.task: Provide detailed analysis per applicable standards and codes.

test_cases.name: Cold Weather Pipeline Operation
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: Low temperature concerns: brittle fracture susceptibility, decompression limits per CSA Z662, Joule-Thomson cooling (-7°F per 1,000 psi drop), hydrate formation (temperature above hydrate curve required), viscosity increase for liquids. Materials: Charpy V-notch impact testing temperature, transition curve shift. Protection: methanol/glycol injection for hydrate inhibition, insulation on above-ground pipe, heat tracing for instrumentation, freeze protection for valves and instruments.
test_cases.task: Provide detailed analysis per applicable standards and codes.

test_cases.name: Inertial Pigging Design
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: Inertial pig (foam or unsealed) for dewaxing: velocity 5-15 ft/sec, bypass ports to prevent stuck conditions, cup design for scraping, magnet for MFL tools, transmitter for tracking. Launcher design for inertial pigs (larger bore than pipeline). Velocity control via back pressure. Cleaning efficiency 70-90% typically. Multiple pigs in train for heavy deposits. Barrels 2× pipe length minimum.
test_cases.task: Provide detailed analysis per applicable standards and codes.

test_cases.name: Hydrotest Water Treatment
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: Hydrotest water specification: pH 5-8, chlorides <50 ppm for stainless, suspended solids <50 ppm, oxygen <1 ppm if stainless present. Treatment: filtration, pH adjustment with sodium carbonate, biocide addition if hold >3 days, corrosion inhibitor 200-500 ppm for carbon steel. Disposal: settlement ponds, pH adjustment, discharge permit compliance, NPDES or state specific. Drying: air displacement with methanol slug, nitrogen displacement, vacuum drying.
test_cases.task: Provide detailed analysis per applicable standards and codes.

test_cases.name: Pipeline Capacity Optimization
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: Capacity increase options: loop lines (parallel segment), line pack utilization (pressure increase), drag reduction agents (0.5-2% throughput increase, ongoing cost), compressor station addition (incremental), dehydration bypass (if specification allows), MAOP increase (if test data supports). Economic analysis: incremental cost vs revenue, net present value, payback period. Constraints: class location limiting MAOP, compression power availability.
test_cases.task: Provide detailed analysis per applicable standards and codes.

test_cases.name: Vortex Shedding Analysis
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: Helmholtz resonator or vortex shedding in thermowells and probes: Strouhal number S = f×D/V, critical velocity when shedding frequency matches natural frequency of structure. Prevention: helical strakes, shrouds, increased stiffness, reduced length/diameter ratio, turbulence promotion. Piping: Spans susceptible if f_shedding ≈ f_span. Damping via snubbers. Thermowell design: ASME PTC 19.3 TW for wake frequency calculation.
test_cases.task: Provide detailed analysis per applicable standards and codes.

test_cases.name: Pipeline River Crossing Hydrology
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: 100-year flood elevation determination: FEMA maps or site-specific analysis. Scour depth calculation: CSU or HEC-18 methods, typically 2-3 feet in cohesive soils, 5-10+ in non-cohesive. Countermeasures: riprap, articulating concrete blocks, sheet piling, guide banks. Depth of burial: below predicted scour depth plus 2 feet minimum. Directional drill preferred over open cut in flood-prone areas. Monitoring: settlement gauges after construction.
test_cases.task: Provide detailed analysis per applicable standards and codes.

test_cases.name: Facility Siting Per EP Guidelines
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: NFPA 59A for LNG, OSHA PSM for high hazard. Siting: occupied building distances, property line clearances, fire protection water supply, access for emergency response, prevailing wind for vapor dispersion. Blast radius: from maximum credible event, overpressure 0.5 psi for buildings. Toxic release: ERPG-2 concentration at property line. Radiation: 5,000 Btu/ft²/hr at property line. Quantitative Risk Assessment for optimized siting.
test_cases.task: Provide detailed analysis per applicable standards and codes.

test_cases.name: Pipeline Integrity Management Plan
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: IMP per 192 Subpart O: baseline assessment within 10 years, reassessment every 7 years maximum, five-year schedule, HCAs identified per 192.5, threats addressed (external corrosion, internal, SCC, third-party, material, equipment), prevention and mitigation measures, performance metrics, communication plan, management of change, quality assurance. Continuous integrity assessment vs periodic.
test_cases.task: Provide detailed analysis per applicable standards and codes.

test_cases.name: Meter Sizing Application
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: Orifice meter: beta ratio 0.2-0.7 optimal (0.25-0.5 typical), permanent pressure loss 2-4× differential, turndown 3:1 typical, accuracy ±0.5% if calibrated range. Ultrasonic: path configuration (single-path 2-3% accuracy, multi-path 0.5%), straight run requirements (10D upstream, 5D downstream), turndown 100:1. Coriolis: accuracy 0.1-0.2%, liquid or dense gas only. Selection: fluid properties, flow range, accuracy requirement, maintenance access, cost lifecycle.
test_cases.task: Provide detailed analysis per applicable standards and codes.

test_cases.name: Pipeline Loop Line Sizing
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: Loop line design: parallel segment reduces flow resistance. Loop line length fraction determined by required flow increase. Hydraulic calculation: equivalent resistance of parallel lines lower than either alone. Sizing: typically equal diameter to mainline, shorter length to achieve 10-20% capacity increase. Pig trap sizing: accommodate both main and loop. Valve arrangement: bypass to isolate either line. Tie-in: hot tap or shutdown. Economic: cost of loop vs compressor station.
test_cases.task: Provide detailed analysis per applicable standards and codes.

test_cases.name: Hydraulic Analysis Software
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: Pipeline simulation: steady-state for capacity rating, transient for line pack and surge analysis, optimization for compressor scheduling. Common software: PipelineStudio (SPS), OLGA for multiphase, PIPESYS. Data requirements: pipe segments with elevation profile, diameter, wall, roughness, stations (compressors, heaters, valves), boundary conditions, fluid composition. Calibration: field data matching (pressure, flow, temperature). Sensitivity: what-if scenarios for operations.
test_cases.task: Provide detailed analysis per applicable standards and codes.

test_cases.name: Pressure Regulation Station
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: Pressure regulation: monitor regulators (active/standby), pilot-operated valves, slam-shuts on high/low pressure. Sizing: Cv calculation for maximum flow at minimum inlet. Turndown: 10:1 typical, wider with dual-range. Accuracy: ±1% of setpoint. Response time: 1-2 seconds. Filter upstream: 40 micron typical. Relief protection downstream. Station piping: minimum bends, supports for weight, thermal expansion loops. Emergency response: full close time from control signal. SCADA: pressure trending, alarms on deviation.
test_cases.task: Provide detailed analysis per applicable standards and codes.

test_cases.name: Pipeline Recommissioning
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: Recommissioning after mothball: inspection prior to return (external, ILI if long outage). Integrity verification: pressure test per original design or 192.619. Cleaning: pigging to remove deposits, dehydration if required. Purging: air removal for gas, inert gas (nitrogen) displacement. Valves: cycle testing, seat tightness. Instruments: calibration. Corrosion: inhibitor injection if internal. Cathodic protection: current requirement verification. Documentation: recommissioning checklist, as-left configurations.
test_cases.task: Provide detailed analysis per applicable standards and codes.

test_cases.name: Seismic Design Criteria
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: ASCE 7 seismic design: spectral acceleration Ss and S1 from USGS maps. Site class (A-F) from soil properties. Design spectrum: short period and 1-second acceleration. Pipeline strain: longitudinal from wave propagation, transverse from PGV. Design: fault crossing accommodation (flexible joints, offset), liquefaction mitigation (deep burial, soil improvement), landslide avoidance or stabilization. ASME B31.8 Case N-755 for seismic. Emergency shutdown on seismic trigger (earthquake early warning).
test_cases.task: Provide detailed analysis per applicable standards and codes.

test_cases.name: Meter Proving
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: Meter prover: pipe prover (displacement) or compact prover (piston). Prover volume: 10,000× meter resolution minimum for 0.05% uncertainty. Master meter method as alternative. Temperature compensation: 1°F = 0.3% volume change. Pressure: compressibility factor. Runs: minimum 5 consecutive within 0.05% repeatability. Frequency: annually or per contract. Factor calculation: prover volume / meter indicated. Uncertainty: 0.1% typical for pipe prover.
test_cases.task: Provide detailed analysis per applicable standards and codes.

test_cases.name: Pipeline Construction Inspection
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: Construction inspection phases: clearing and grading, trenching, stringing, welding (100% of welds inspected X-ray or UT), joint coating, lowering in, backfill, testing, cleanup. Welding: WPS compliance, NDE acceptance, repair procedures. Coating: holiday testing 100%. Lowering: stress analysis, bending radius, sling protection. Backfill: material selection, compaction density. Test: hydrostatic, leak detection, pressure holding. Documentation: daily reports, as-built drawings, test records.
test_cases.task: Provide detailed analysis per applicable standards and codes.

test_cases.name: Pipeline Capacity Assessment
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: Maximum operating capacity not always equal to nameplate: MAOP limit, compressor station power, temperature constraints, customer nominations. Capacity model: actual vs theoretical. Bottleneck identification: station with highest pressure ratio, pipeline segment with most friction. Debottlenecking: loop lines, compressor upgrades, drag reducers, MAOP increase. Seasonal capacity: winter demand peaks. Interruptible vs firm service. Storage injection/withdrawal impact on capacity booking.
test_cases.task: Provide detailed analysis per applicable standards and codes.

test_cases.name: Ground Bed Anode Design
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: Deep anode bed: 100+ feet depth for low resistance, multiple tubular anodes in coke breeze backfill. Design: 50 Amp typical output, resistance less than 0.5 Ohm, 20-year design life. Anode consumption: 1 lb/Amp-year. Cable sizing: voltage drop calculation, HMWPE insulation rated for chlorine if applicable. Vent pipes for gas release. Monitoring: individual anode current measurement. Shallow ground bed: 20-50 feet spacing, graphite or HSCI anodes, horizontal or vertical. Soil resistivity testing for location.
test_cases.task: Provide detailed analysis per applicable standards and codes.

test_cases.name: Pipeline Abandonment
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: Abandonment per 192.727: purge of hazardous liquids or gas (inert gas displacement), fill with water or inert material if potential contamination. Below grade discontinuation: cut and cap, or pull. Cathodic protection: disconnect and document. Environmental: remove if surface or water crossing, address contamination. Records: location remains in database. Notification: landowners, state agencies (3-6 months advance), PHMSA if hazardous line. Easement: review status, some require pipe removal per agreement.
test_cases.task: Provide detailed analysis per applicable standards and codes.
